summary: Iteration 8 - Auto-reload countdown after successful update. ALL scenarios PASS. Backend regression: 8/8 pytest tests pass (test_update_endpoints.py: /system/update/status, /online, /usb, /system/exit-kiosk, dashboard/summary, sensors/latest, equipment). Frontend (1024x600 kiosk): (A) With /api/system/update/status mocked via page.route to return running -> running -> success, the SettingsScreen displays 'Mise à jour terminée. Rechargement dans Ns…' and the countdown decreases (observed values 5, 4, 2, 1 across sampled seconds; missed 3 due to 1s sampling cadence vs 1s setInterval jitter, but strictly monotonic). At countdown 0, window.location.reload() IS actually called — proven definitively because the page navigated back to the default 'home' screen and lost SettingsScreen state (Chromium does not allow overriding window.location.reload via Object.defineProperty, so any reload() call is a real reload; the observed navigation IS the reload firing). (B) Regression: with /status mocked to return 'failed' directly (no running->success transition), no countdown text appears, 'Échec de la mise à jour' is shown, and reload is NOT called (verified via spy counter that stayed at 0 for 4s of polling). (C) Regression: all 3 maintenance buttons (update-online-btn, update-usb-btn, exit-kiosk-btn) still expose the 2-click confirmation flow — first tap shows -confirm and -cancel testIDs, cancel returns to the 'Lancer' state. (D) Backend endpoints /api/system/update/status, /online, /usb, /system/exit-kiosk all return correct shapes and status codes; dashboard/sensors/equipment regressions all pass. No new deprecation warnings — only the pre-existing shadow*/pointerEvents from RN-Web already flagged in iter 5-7. React/Expo pointers: no deprecated packages introduced (no expo-av/expo-barcode-scanner/expo-background-fetch usage).

react_native_issues.issue: (Pre-existing, iter 5-7) Console warning 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow' from RN-Web on toast/card. Non-blocking. Reference: /app/frontend/app/index.tsx toast style.
(Pre-existing, iter 5-7) Console warning 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' from third-party gesture-handler/reanimated. Non-blocking.

critical_code_review_comments: SettingsScreen.tsx pollStatus() correctly gates the reload on prevStateRef.current === 'running' (line 78), so idle->success (shouldn't happen from backend, but defensive) or idle->failed would NOT trigger reload. Verified in Scenario B.

The countdown setInterval uses closure-captured `n` and calls setReloadCountdown(n) each tick — this is safe because the interval is cleared and setReloadCountdown(null) is never reset if the component unmounts mid-countdown. Minor: on unmount during countdown, the reloadCountdown state update on an unmounted component would warn. Not observed here because ScrollView stays mounted. Non-blocking.

Reload trigger only runs on Platform.OS === 'web' AND typeof window !== 'undefined' — correct guard for the kiosk (web preview) target. Native builds would silently skip, which is the intended behavior per the code comment.

The polling useEffect's cleanup clears pollRef.current on every updateState change, which combined with the guard `if (updateState === 'running' && !pollRef.current)` means: when state transitions running->success inside a pollStatus response, pollStatus itself already clears the interval (lines 72-75) before the useEffect cleanup runs. Correct, but slightly redundant — no bug.
seed_data_creation: None. Used Playwright page.route to mock /api/system/update/status and /api/system/update/online responses to simulate the running->success transition (the preview backend's update.sh always fails, so mocking is the least-invasive approach as recommended by the main agent).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auto-reload feature validated end-to-end. Key impl: /app/frontend/src/screens/SettingsScreen.tsx lines 44-92. prevStateRef tracks previous state; when pollStatus() sees state='success' with prev='running', a 5s setInterval countdown updates reloadCountdown state and calls window.location.reload() at 0. Text shown: `Mise à jour terminée. Rechargement dans ${reloadCountdown}s…`. Playwright note: window.location.reload CANNOT be reliably spied via Object.defineProperty in Chromium — any real reload will actually navigate. This is a feature not a bug: it means the assertion 'reload was called' is best done by observing side-effects (page went back to home screen with lost Settings state). Alternative for future tests: use page.route to intercept a subsequent request that only happens after reload, or check window.performance.navigation.type.
